title: Gobi Masala

ingredients.title: For the Masala (Gravy)
items: 1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)
1/2 tsp amchur (dry mango powder) or 1 tsp lemon juice
Fresh coriander leaves, chopped for garnish

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the Cauliflower
steps: Wash and cut the cauliflower into bite-sized florets.
In a bowl, toss florets with turmeric, red chili powder, and a pinch of salt.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan on medium heat. Lightly sauté the cauliflower until it’s slightly golden but not fully cooked. Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: 2. Make the Masala (Gravy)
steps: In a heavy-bottomed pan, heat 3 tbsp oil.
Add cumin seeds; when they splutter, add onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste, cook for 1–2 minutes until raw smell disappears.
Add tomato puree, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder. Cook until oil separates from the masala (about 5–7 minutes).
Add water to make a thick gravy.

instruction.title: 3. Cook the Gobi (Cauliflower) in the Masala
steps: Add sautéed cauliflower to the masala. Mix gently.
Cover and cook on low heat for 8–10 minutes until cauliflower is tender but not mushy.
Sprinkle garam masala and amchur powder (or lemon juice) at the end. Mix gently.

instruction.title: 4. Garnish and Serve
steps: Serve hot with naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice.

chefTips: Golden Color: Blanch cauliflower in hot water with a pinch of turmeric before frying for brighter color.
Flavor Depth: Roast whole spices (cumin, coriander seeds) and grind them fresh to boost aroma.
Creamy Twist: For richer texture, add 2 tbsp cream or cashew paste at the end.
Smoky Flavor: Use the traditional “dhungar” method: place a small piece of hot charcoal in a bowl, put it in the pan with ghee, and cover to infuse a subtle smoky aroma.
